title: Pilocereus rhodacanthus
intro: Pilocereus rhodacanthus, also known as the red-flowered torch cactus, is a species of cactus native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits such as antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Pilocereus rhodacanthus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pilocereus rhodacanthus is commonly used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory infections, fever, and inflammation.
taxonomy: Pilocereus rhodacanthus is a species of cactus in the family Cactaceae. It is a type of columnar cactus native to Mexico. The classification of Pilocereus rhodacanthus includes its genus Pilocereus, which is part of the tribe Pachycereae. Pachycereae is a subtribe within the larger family Cactaceae. Pilocereus rhodacanthus is also sometimes classified under the subfamily Cactoideae.

Its medicinal uses include treating conditions such as arthritis, digestive issues, and skin problems.
